news.t: Market Extra: Here are some ETFs for Black Friday
Online retail stocks are expensive 'no matter how you slice it,' researcher says. Here's his top play
E-commerce ETFs rally sharply as bricks-and-mortar underwhelms

holdings.infoBox: EBIZ is an equity ETF with a total of 42 individual holdings. The top holdings are eBay Inc. stock at 5.61%, Williams-Sonoma at 5.23%, Etsy, Inc. at 5.14%, Amazon.com at 5.04%, and RB Global at 4.83%.

overview.description: The Global X E-Commerce ETF (EBIZ) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Solactive E-commerce index, a market-cap-weighted index of global e-commerce companies, including online retailers, retail platforms, and supporting businesses. EBIZ was launched on Nov 27, 2018 and is issued by Global X.

data.title: Market Extra: Here are some ETFs for Black Friday
data.text: A roundup of exchange-traded funds that will help you invest in e-commerce plays.

data.title: Online retail stocks are expensive 'no matter how you slice it,' researcher says. Here's his top play
data.text: No two funds are exactly alike in the skyrocketing e-commerce space, ETF analysts Dave Nadig and Chris Hempstead say.

data.title: Online retail ETFs: Overbought or prime for the picking?
data.text: Chris Hempstead, director of institutional business development at IndexIQ, and Dave Nadig, chief investment officer and director of research at ETF Trends and ETF Database, discuss winners and losers...

data.title: Global X: There are huge consumer spending categories that have yet to shift online
data.text: Jay Jacobs of Global X ETFs discusses how there are still massive opportunities for e-commerce to grow beyond the traditional categories of electronics and clothing.

data.title: E-commerce ETFs rally sharply as bricks-and-mortar underwhelms
data.text: E-commerce focused exchange-traded funds jumped Tuesday as a fresh round of updates from big retailers confirmed more consumer activity was moving online.
